description: A lab suitable for investigating alien xenolife, planetary geology, esoteric technology, and other mysteries. Skill rolls improved by +1 (frigate), +2 (cruiser), or +3 (capital).
attribute: Skill bonus for analysis and research

description: Reduces the DC of all Pilot/Nav checks made during travel segments by 2.
attribute: +2 for Pilot/Nav checks during travel segments

description: Includes Atmospheric Configuration benefits plus the ability to operate while immersed in a liquid medium. Only fighter and frigate hulls.
attribute: Can land and can operate under water

description: Stores one cell of antimatter. Has active magnetic confinement. At 0 Power, containment fails in 1d6 rounds.
Ships so equipped have whatever amounts of TL4 military-grade weaponry and armor that a crew might require, and integral maintenance facilities for its upkeep.
attribute: Weapons and armor for the crew

description: Must be put in place when the ship is built. Cannot be installed on cruiser-class or larger ships. A ship designed for atmospheric flight can land on most solid or aqueous surfaces.
attribute: Can land: frigates and fighters only

description: Installing an autonomic targeting system for a gun allows it to shoot at a +2 hit bonus without human assistance. Must be installed once for each gun to be self-manned.
Ship has been fitted to support non-sentient expert system robots in its operation. At least one human crew member is necessary. Bots cost 1,000 credits per crew member replaced.
Armored tubes equipped with laser cutter apertures can be used to forcibly invade a hostile ship, provided the target's engines have been disabled.
Orbit-to-surface shuttle for cruisers and larger craft that can't land. Can carry up to 200 tons of cargo and passengers.
Pressurized cargo space. One mass = 2 tons (fighter), 20 tons (frigate), 200 tons (cruiser), 2000 tons (capital).
Stasis pods that keep subjects alive for centuries. Each installation keeps a number of people equal to the ship's maximum crew in stasis indefinitely.
Ship can be deconstructed into a colony base supporting up to five times its maximum crew. Once activated, the ship ceases to be operational and the core cannot be re-packed.
A fuel bunker, but cryo-cooled. Prevents hydrogen and deuterium-helium-3 boil-off. Also necessary for long-term storage of nuclear saltwater.
Armored and stealthed cargo lighters, twice as fast, -3 penalty to tracking/targeting. Can carry up to one hundred troops.
Stealth systems that mask the ship's energy emissions. TWR cut in half when engaged, but +2 bonus to detection avoidance skill checks.
Compressed cold sleep pods for enormous numbers of colonists. Cruiser: 1,000 colonists, capital: 5,000. Each additional installation doubles the number.
Each installation increases the maximum crew rating by 100% of its normal maximum.
Improved medical facilities allowing treatment of up to the ship's entire maximum crew at once, including critically wounded passengers.
Each selection doubles the maximum life support duration (normally two months for max crew).
Stores 5 units of fissionable particles. Shielded, reinforced, and stamped with a DAVE compliance serial number.
Stores 5 units of a single fuel type. Fuel types cannot be mixed.
Allows atmospheric skimming of Argon or Hydrogen. Refuelling 1 cell takes one travel segment, plus a Piloting check (DC 12 for gas giants, DC 10 for planetary atmospheres).
Produces food and air supplies for a number of crewmen equal to the ship's maximum crew. Each additional selection doubles the supported population.
Synthesises deuterium-helium-3 fuel cells. Produces 1 cell per week of continuous operation. Requires the ship to be stationary (or in a stable parking orbit) and a working Fuel Scoop.
Single-use escape craft capable of reaching the nearest habitable planet or station. Provides lifeboats for a ship's maximum crew.
Each selection provides luxurious quarters for 10% of the ship's maximum crew.
Automated mining and refinery equipment. Can refine one ton of materials per day worth ~500 credits. Requires at least five crew members to operate.
Full-scale TL4 fabrication plant. Can stock raw materials at 5,000 credits per ton to pay ship repair/maintenance costs. Creates/repairs at 10,000 credits per day.
Disguise a ship's long-range sensor readings with the ID tags and hull type of another ship. Penetrated by Wis/Program vs difficulty 10 + comms officer's Program skill.
A docking bay with all necessary tools and support for launching a fighter-class starship.
A docking bay with all necessary tools and support for launching a frigate-class starship.
General supply of TL4 equipment likely useful to explorers and spacemen. Some basic guns and armor may be included.
Allows one craft of a hull size smaller than the tender to link up for transit. Docking/releasing: 1 (fighter), 2 (frigate), 3 (cruiser) rounds, during which both vessels are immobile.
Hidden cargo space: 200kg (fighter), 2 tons (frigate), 20 tons (cruiser), 200 tons (capital). Never found by standard customs inspection.
Greatly enhanced sensor abilities for detailed mapping and broad-spectrum communications analysis. +2 to skill checks when scanning.
A turreted electromagnetic tether system to push, pull, and slide objects no larger than a ship of one hull class smaller. Can move objects into cargo bays within three rounds.
Specialized mounts, bunking, and cargo bays for vehicles. Carried vehicles count as only half their usual cargo tonnage.
Automated tech workshops. Frigate-sized = personal gear modding, cruiser-sized = vehicle modding and ship maintenance, capital = build vehicles from scratch.
